label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me read this question and figure out the answer. The question is about why Smith became rich. The passage says, "Smith was a landlord. He loved money very much and never gave anything to anybody. Soon he became rich." So the reason he became rich is because he didn't give anything to others. The sentence in the question is: "Smith became very rich, because he _ gave anything to anybody." The blank needs a word that fits with the idea of not giving anything. The options are (A) always, (B) never, (C) often, (D) sometimes. Looking back at the passage, it clearly states he "never gave anything to anybody." That matches option B. The other options don't fit. If he always gave, he wouldn't be rich. Often or sometimes giving would mean he did share some, but the story says he didn't give at all. The story also ends with
